name: JuddHome
description: Netflix/Disney+ style home screen with intelligent recommendations for Juddflix
overview: Replaces the Jellyfin home screen with modular Netflix-style rows including Because You Watched, Hero Banner, and personalised recommendations based on watch history. Supports per-user layout preferences across multiple users.

versions.changelog: Fixes the 1.0.8.0 cleanup task having no way to run it: a scheduled task with zero triggers doesn't get a Run Now control in Jellyfin's Scheduled Tasks page. 'Remove Studio Collections (JuddHome, one-time cleanup)' now has a startup trigger, so it runs automatically the next time the server starts (which you need to do anyway to pick up this update) with no manual clicking needed. Safe to run repeatedly — it's a no-op once the studio collections are gone.

Removes the studio Collections auto-grouping (was sweeping nearly every movie into a collection) and adds a one-time 'Remove Studio Collections (JuddHome, one-time cleanup)' scheduled task (Dashboard > Scheduled Tasks, Run Now) to delete the ones it already made, without touching your movies/shows or any other collection. Adds a 'Because You Watched: max rows' setting (1-5, default 3) in Settings, and moves it near the bottom of the default section order. Also fixes the admin Settings page, which was missing Just Added Movies/TV Shows entirely (added in 1.0.6.0 but never wired into the settings page) — they're now visible and reorderable there too.

Fixes a caching bug that has silently affected every release since 1.0.1.0: the injected home screen <script> tag never changed its URL between versions, so browsers and TV apps kept serving whatever home.js/home.css/home.html they first loaded and never picked up later client-side fixes (including the Tizen fetch() fix and the 1.0.6.0 Collections/Genres/Decades tabs). The script tag now carries a ?v=<version> marker that changes on every update, and the asset endpoint now sends Cache-Control: no-store. If the home screen still looks unchanged after updating to this version, a hard refresh (or clearing the browser/app cache once) should finally pick up everything from 1.0.2.0 through 1.0.6.0 that got stuck.

Adds a JuddHome-owned tab row (Home | Collections | Genres | Decades) below the header on the home screen: Collections shows every Collection in the library, Genres and Decades show every genre/decade with unwatched titles, full lists not capped like the home rows. Also fixes 'Latest Movies'/'Latest TV Shows' to sort by release date instead of when the file was added to the server, and adds two new sections, 'Just Added Movies' and 'Just Added TV Shows', for the old added-to-server sort behaviour.

Adds two new home screen row sections, Browse By Genre and Browse By Decade, built from unwatched-pool metadata already available (no configuration required). Also adds automatic Studio Collections: a new 'Organize Studio Collections (JuddHome)' scheduled task (Dashboard > Scheduled Tasks, category JuddHome) groups movies/shows sharing a studio into real Jellyfin Collections, runs daily by default or on demand via Run Now, and only creates a collection once a studio has 3+ items.
Adds a 'Once a day' (24h) option to the recommendation refresh interval dropdown in Settings — previously capped at every 12 hours.

Perf: recommendation and Because You Watched caches are now proactively re-warmed for every user right after each scheduled profile refresh, in the background, instead of being computed on-demand during a user's own page load. Previously the per-request cache only helped repeat visits within 3 minutes of each other, so most real visits (more than 3 minutes apart) still paid the full library-scoring cost live. Cache lifetime now tracks the configured recommendation refresh interval instead of a fixed 3 minutes.

Perf: cache the unwatched-item pool, Because You Watched rows, and Recommended For You results per user for 3 minutes, instead of rescoring the whole library on every request. Fix: home screen failed to load on Samsung Tizen TV app because the template loader used fetch(), which some Tizen WebViews don't implement — replaced with XMLHttpRequest.
Fix: JuddHome Settings modal never closed (Cancel/Save appeared to do nothing and blocked all interaction) due to a CSS rule that overrode the browser's default hidden-attribute behaviour.
